actionable_feedback: Ensure the spreadsheet contains live formulas (not hard-coded values) for the eq-min calculations and remediation transformations, and make those cells easily discoverable (explicit row-level cell references for R1/R2/R3).

Add a verification sheet in the workbook named VERIFICATION (or HOW_TO_VERIFY) that lists key formulas and the exact cell locations used for each record's eq-min and for scenario transforms (e.g., R1_eqmin = <cell formula>, O1_temperature_formula = <cell formula>), so verifiers can validate formulas even if automated XLSX parsing is unavailable.

Demonstrate the 'needs_review' workflow in the spreadsheet (e.g., include a sample row or a flag column showing how ambiguous items will be flagged) so verifiers can confirm that review markers are produced by the workbook.
Add a small cost breakdown table in the memo and spreadsheet (cost per option, sum check) and explicitly state the currency/unit used for costs.

In the memo, explicitly document the scope of the semantic score (i.e., whether semantic correctness claims are conditioned on memo + normalized scenario values versus workbook formula verification). Clarify that the current semantic assessment is conditioned on memo/scenario agreement and that full workbook-level semantic verification requires formula inspection.
Add a brief rationale (1-2 sentences) supporting the recommendation ordering (e.g., why O2 is preferred under conservatism) and reference sensitivity-check results.

Re-run automated checks (or request verifier re-run) after enabling openpyxl in the preview environment or after adding the VERIFICATION sheet, and include a one-line statement in the memo that confirms the workbook contains live formulas and the verification sheet is present.
feedback_summary: Baseline submission is complete and internally consistent: memo, spreadsheet, scenario normalization, and remediation tables are present and match. Grader and verifier both mark passed, but the verifier raised two actionable warnings: (1) the XLSX could not be programmatically inspected (openpyxl unavailable), so formula-level verification is not confirmed; (2) the grader reported full semantic correctness despite the deterministic precheck noting semantics were not programmatically evaluated. Addressing those warnings will improve reproducibility and reduce overconfidence in semantic scoring.

evidence_used: attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] (spreadsheet file present; preview unavailable for formulas)

limitations: Unable to programmatically open/inspect the spreadsheet (openpyxl not available); spreadsheet-formula-level verification relied on the memo and normalized scenario metadata.

verifier_issues.title: Unable to verify spreadsheet formulas (openpyxl unavailable)

verifier_issues.message: Grader gave semantic_score=1.0 while deterministic precheck notes semantic correctness was not programmatically evaluated. This is an overconfident claim unless explicitly scoped to memo+scenario agreement.
verifier_issues.suggested_fix: Document semantic-scoring scope in the memo and/or enable workbook-level inspection to justify semantic claims.
verifier_issues.title: Semantic score asserted despite deterministic precheck not evaluating semantics
